scenario_description: A user documents the preparation of a pasta dish, tracking a specific red bell pepper from whole to diced to garnish amidst other vegetables like zucchini and carrots.

turns.text: Here's my prep station. I'm chopping veggies for dinner.
turns.image_description: A top-down view of a wooden cutting board under bright kitchen lighting. On the left sits a large, glossy, whole red bell pepper with green stems attached. In the center, there is a pile of diced orange carrots. On the right, a halved green zucchini rests next to a knife. The background shows a blurred kitchen counter.

turns.text: Looks like a colorful start! That red pepper looks really fresh. Are you planning to roast it or use it raw?
I'm dicing everything up. Here is the bowl ready for the pan.
turns.image_description: A close-up shot of a clear glass mixing bowl containing mixed diced vegetables. There are small cubes of orange carrots, pale green zucchini chunks, and bright red cubes of bell pepper. The red cubes are distinct and uniform in size, contrasting with the other vegetables. A wooden spoon rests on the rim of the bowl.

turns.text: Nice uniform cuts. Those red cubes will add a great pop of color to the sauce. Did you save any of the skins?
Finished plating! What do you think?
turns.image_description: A white ceramic plate holding a serving of creamy fettuccine pasta. Topped generously with the cooked vegetable mixture seen previously: orange carrot pieces and green zucchini bits are scattered throughout. Resting prominently on the very top center of the pasta mound is a single, thin, curved slice of roasted red pepper skin, acting as a garnish. The lighting is warm and inviting.

turns.text: That looks delicious! The garnish really ties the whole plate together.
Which vegetable was used for the garnish on top of the pasta? A) The orange carrots B) The green zucchini C) The red bell pepper D) A new ingredient not shown before

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 introduces a whole red bell pepper, orange carrots, and green zucchini. 2. Turn 3 shows these three items diced into a bowl; the red cubes correspond to the red bell pepper. 3. Turn 5 shows the final dish where the main body contains diced orange and green vegetables (carrots and zucchini), but the specific garnish on top is a slice of red pepper skin. 4. By cross-referencing the color and form across all turns, the garnish is identified as the red bell pepper introduced in Turn 1.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the 'red bell pepper' entity across three distinct visual states (whole fruit, diced cubes, roasted skin garnish) while distinguishing it from the other two recurring vegetables (carrots and zucchini) which appear in similar diced forms in the middle turn but are not used as the specific top garnish. A model relying only on the final image might guess 'new ingredient' or confuse the colors if it doesn't recall the initial whole vegetable context.

_meta.scenario: A user follows a recipe where a single red bell pepper is first shown whole, then diced into small cubes in a bowl, and finally appears as a garnish on top of a finished pasta dish. The images also show other vegetables being prepared simultaneously.
